title: Combinatorial Purged Cross-Validation (CPCV) for ML Engine
summary: Replace the simple chronological train/test split in ml_engine.py with Combinatorial Purged Cross-Validation (CPCV), as developed by Marcos Lopez de Prado (Advances in Financial Machine Learning). CPCV prevents information leakage in two ways: (1) it 'purges' training observations whose triple-barrier labels overlap with test period dates, and (2) it adds an 'embargo' — a gap of N bars after each test fold before the next training fold starts. A 2025 ScienceDirect paper comparing CV methods for financial ML found CPCV has the lowest PBO score across all tested approaches, outperforming standard k-fold, time-series split, and purged k-fold. Critical for Decifer because the existing meta-labeling and triple barrier labels inherently overlap in time.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — Backtesting Robustness, Walk-Forward Validation & Intraday Timing Signals
expected_impact: +2–4% OOS win rate by eliminating label leakage in triple barrier and meta-labeling training; ScienceDirect 2025 paper shows CPCV has materially lower Probability of Backtest Overfitting (PBO) vs all other CV methods tested
deferral_reason: ML training methodology, future — deferred during paper_trading_single_account prioritisation
